content_en:
(Because what they spent benefited Islam more)

"Allah! Allah! (Keep the thought of Allah) concerning my companions. "Do not take them as a target" (for

your obscenity or charges) after me, He who loves them, loves me but he who hate them, hates Me, and he

who offends them, offends me and in turn offends Allah and he who offends Allah, Allah almost takes him"2

"The sign of faith is love of the helpers (Ansar), and the sign of hypocrisy is hatred of the helpers."3

This is because they were the first believers who fought the enemies of Islam fiercely at the lifetime of the

Prophet (pbuh). Likewise, the love of Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) is of faith and the hatred of him

The excellence of the Prophet's companions can be recognized by studying their biography and their

traces during the Prophet's lifetime and thereafter. This excellence is represented in their sincere faith,

fighting against the disbelievers, disseminating the belief, declaring the Islamic rites, raising high the word

of Allah and His Messenger and teaching others the obligatory and Sunna worshiping. Were not they, we

would not receive anything concerning the religion. Hence, whoever disparages them is beyond the pale of

Such disparaging is undoubtedly out of the belief that they were wicked people and thus denying the

sayings of Allah and His Messenger when praising them and indicating their virtues. They are also the

intermediaries between the source and the target of sacred knowledge. Doubting the intermediary

"Allah has selected me and picked out my companions. From among my Companions, I have ministers,

helpers, and relatives by marriage. Thereby, whoever disparages them entails the curse of Allah, His

angels and all the people. Allah will not accept their deeds on the Day of Judgment."4

Anas Ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) related, once the Prophetic Companions said,

"The curse of Allah the angels and the whole people is upon whoever reviles my companions." 5

"Allah has selected me and picked out my companions. He also granted me friends, brothers and relatives

by marriage. There will be another generation thereafter who will revile and disparage them. Therefore,

Reported by AI-Bukhari and Muslim.

3. Repoted by AI-Bukhari and Muslim.

Reported by Ibn Hajar AI-Haithami in Majma' Al-Zawa'id.

Reported by Ibn Hajar AI-Haithami in Majma' Al-Zawa'id.

you should not interact with them in food, drink marriage, prayer over them or prayer with them." 1

Ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) related that the Prophet (pbuh) said,

"Hold your tongue whenever you hear the mentioning of my Companions, stars and destiny."2

Scholars argued that, holding one's tongue is a sign of complete submission to Allah.

It is obligatory for a Muslim to love Allah and his Messenger. Of the Prophet's love is to love all what

He was sent with, those who take after him, those who apply his tradition and guidance, household,

companions, wives, progeny, servants, and to love whoever loves them and hate whoever hates them.

Abu Ayyub As-Sakhtiyyani (may Allah be pleased with him) said, whoever loves Abu Bakr raises the

minaret of the religion higher, whoever loves 'Umar clarifies the way, whoever loves 'Uthman, is en-

lightened by Allah's light, whoever loves Ali adheres to the clearest principle and whoever says, the

Prophet's companions are good and right is free from hypocrisy.

As for the virtues of the Prophetic companions, they are countless. The best of the companions are the

ten whom the Prophet promised to enter paradise. The best four of them are Abu Bakr, 'Umar, 'Uthman and'

Ali (may Allah be pleased with them all). Whoever doubts them, is a hypocrite.

In the hadith narrated by AI-'Irbad Ibn Sariyyah, the Prophet said,

"You must follow my deeds (Sunna) and that of the rightly guided caliphs. Hold it and stick fast to it. Avoid

novelties (in religious matters), for every novelty is an innovation and every innovation is an error and

every error is in hell." 3

The Rightly-Guided Caliphs are: Abu Bakr, 'Umar, 'Uthman and , Ali. The former was mentioned in

"Let not those among you who are endued with grace and amplitude of means resolve by oath against

helping their kinsmen, those in want, and those who have left their homes in Allah's cause"

{ Being the second of the two, they two were in the care.} (AtTawbah: 40)

{And he who brings the truth and who confirms (and supports) it.} (Az-Zummar: 33)

Ash-Shawkani attributed it to Aqily in Durr As-Sahabah and said it is a weak hadith.

As-Suyuti attributed it to AtTabarani in Jami' Al-Adhkar.
content_id:
Tentang Menghina Para Sahabat Nabi ﷺ:

Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Allah! Allah! (Ingatlah Allah) mengenai para sahabatku. Janganlah kalian menjadikan mereka sebagai sasaran (celaan atau tuduhan) sepeninggalku. Barangsiapa mencintai mereka, maka itu adalah karena kecintaan kepadaku. Barangsiapa membenci mereka, maka itu adalah karena kebencian terhadapku."

Para sahabat adalah orang-orang terbaik yang Allah pilih untuk menemani Nabi-Nya dan menyebarkan risalah Islam. Menghina atau mencela mereka adalah bentuk penghinaan terhadap Islam itu sendiri, karena merekalah yang menyampaikan agama ini kepada kita.

Tentang Hak Para Sahabat atas Kita:

Hak para sahabat atas umat Islam yang datang setelah mereka adalah: mencintai mereka, mendoakan mereka, memuliakan mereka, tidak mencela satupun dari mereka, dan meyakini keadilan mereka dalam meriwayatkan hadis Nabi ﷺ.

Tentang Keadilan Para Sahabat:

Para ulama menyatakan bahwa semua sahabat Nabi ﷺ adalah adil ('udul) dalam meriwayatkan hadis. Ini adalah prinsip yang disepakati oleh ulama hadis. Mencela keadilan para sahabat berarti meragukan kebenaran periwayatan hadis yang menjadi sumber hukum Islam kedua setelah Al-Quran.

Penutup: Semoga Allah melindungi kita dari semua dosa-dosa besar yang disebutkan dalam buku ini, membimbing kita ke jalan yang lurus, mengampuni dosa-dosa kita yang telah lalu, dan menerima taubat kita. Sesungguhnya Allah adalah Maha Pengampun lagi Maha Penyayang.
content_ar:
من كبائر الذنوب: أذى أصحاب رسول الله ﷺ والطعن فيهم والقدح في أعراضهم. وقد قال النبي ﷺ: «الله الله في أصحابي لا تتخذوهم غرضاً بعدي. من أحبّهم فبحبّي أحبّهم، ومن أبغضهم فببغضي أبغضهم، ومن آذاهم فقد آذاني ومن آذاني فقد آذى الله». وهذا وعيد شديد لمن يمسّ الصحابة بسوء.

وقد بيّن العلماء أن محبة الصحابة من أصول الإيمان عند أهل السنة والجماعة لأنهم هم حملة الوحي ونقلة السنة وتلاميذ النبي ﷺ المباشرون. فمن أبغضهم أو طعن في عدالتهم طعن في الوحي الذي حملوه ونقلوه. ومن هنا كان التحذير من إيذائهم بالغاً جداً في كتب العقيدة والفقه.

وللمؤمن في هذا الباب موقف واضح: يُحبّ الصحابة جميعاً ويترضّى عنهم ويُمسك عمّا وقع بينهم من خلاف مع الإيمان بأنهم مجتهدون كلٌّ منهم سعى إلى ما يراه حقاً. ومن أراد أن يُنصف التاريخ فليقرأه بعين المحبة والإنصاف لا بعين التجريح والهدم، وليعلم أن في الطعن بهم هدماً لأساس الدين الذي بنوه بدمائهم وجهدهم وتضحياتهم.
